enforcement.check: CancellationToken is the last parameter in all async method signatures.
CancellationToken parameter has a default value of `default`.
CancellationToken parameter is not declared as nullable (CancellationToken?).
CancellationToken parameter is named `cancellationToken` — not `ct`, `token`, or `cancelToken`.
CancellationToken is forwarded to all downstream async calls — not silently omitted.
cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested() is called before dependency or long-running operations in any method accepting CancellationToken.
When a timeoutSource exists, catch (OperationCanceledException) when (timeoutSource.IsCancellationRequested) precedes plain catch (OperationCanceledException).
OperationCanceledException is not wrapped in any other exception type.
No catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; } block is present when no timeoutSource exists.
All tasks in a Task.WhenAll call receive the same CancellationToken.
Theory MemberData for dependency exception tests includes TimeoutException.
A test asserts that OperationCanceledException propagates as-is — not wrapped.
